mechanism_narrative: Persistently high consumer price inflation erodes real purchasing power and corporate profit margins, leading to a downward re-evaluation of equity valuations and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals a deteriorating labor market and broader economic weakness, which negatively impacts corporate earnings expectations and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the correlation between UNRATE changes and SPX returns is not significantly negative during contractions.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales growth is weak while the Federal Funds Rate is elevated, it indicates that consumer demand is struggling under tight monetary conditions, which negatively impacts corporate revenues and equity prices over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction term between RSAFS growth and FEDFUNDS is not significant.

mechanism_narrative: A strong rebound in housing starts during a post-recession recovery period signals renewed economic activity and consumer confidence, which typically precedes positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon as the recovery gains momentum.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 50% of triggered instances during recovery periods, or HOUST growth shows no predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production indicates robust manufacturing and supply-side activity, which translates to healthy corporate earnings and supports positive S&P 500 returns over a short 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns are not significantly positive during periods of INDPRO growth.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate exceeds a certain threshold, it indicates a restrictive monetary policy stance designed to cool the economy, which typically leads to higher discount rates and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of triggered instances when FEDFUNDS is above 4.0%, or the signal's predictive power diminishes significantly.

mechanism_narrative: Robust job growth (PAYEMS) combined with strong retail sales (RSAFS) indicates a healthy consumer base with both the means and willingness to spend, driving corporate revenues and supporting positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect between PAYEMS and RSAFS growth is not statistically significant.

mechanism_narrative: During a mature economic expansion, sustained real GDP growth above a healthy threshold indicates underlying economic strength that supports corporate profitability and leads to positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 50% of triggered instances during mature expansions, or GDPC1 growth shows no significant positive correlation with returns.
